title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud-based services. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code, potentially leading to data breaches, service disruptions, and financial losses.
recommended_actions: Implement strict access controls and monitor CI/CD pipelines for unauthorized changes or suspicious activity.
Regularly back up critical data and ensure backups are stored in isolated environments to prevent ransomware encryption.
Apply security patches and updates to cloud infrastructure and CI/CD tools to mitigate vulnerabilities.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7041.

title: A security vulnerability has been detected in tufantunc ssh-mcp up to 1.5.0. The affected element...
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in tufantunc ssh-mcp up to 1.5.0. The affected element is the function shell.write of the file src/index.ts. Such manipulation of the argument Description leads to command injection. The attack must be carried out locally. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows local attackers to perform command injection via the shell.write function in src/index.ts, potentially leading to unauthorized system control or data manipulation.
recommended_actions: Immediately restrict local access to systems running tufantunc ssh-mcp up to version 1.5.0.
Apply input validation and sanitization to the shell.write function to prevent command injection.
Monitor the project's GitHub repository for updates or patches and consider contacting the maintainers directly.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in Totolink A8000RU 7.1cu.643_b20200521. This issue affects...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Totolink A8000RU 7.1cu.643_b20200521. This issue affects the function setVpnPassCfg of the file /cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi of the component CGI Handler. The manipulation of the argument pptpPassThru results in os command injection. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Totolink A8000RU allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary OS commands via the CGI handler, potentially leading to full system compromise. Public availability of the exploit increases the likelihood of widespread attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the firmware of Totolink A8000RU devices to the latest version if a patch is available.
Restrict remote access to the device by disabling unnecessary services and enabling a firewall to block unauthorized traffic.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and apply intrusion detection/prevention systems (IDS/IPS) to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title: A weakness has been identified in tufantunc ssh-mcp up to 1.5.0. Impacted is an unknown function...
description: A weakness has been identified in tufantunc ssh-mcp up to 1.5.0. Impacted is an unknown function of the file src/index.ts of the component Command Line Handler. This manipulation causes insufficiently protected credentials. The attack is restricted to local execution. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows local attackers to access insufficiently protected credentials, which could lead to unauthorized access or privilege escalation. However, the attack is restricted to local execution, limiting its scope.
recommended_actions: Update to a patched version of tufantunc ssh-mcp if available, or apply temporary mitigations such as restricting local access to the affected systems.
Review and secure credential storage mechanisms to prevent unauthorized access.
Monitor for further updates or patches from the project maintainers and apply them promptly.

description: A vulnerability was identified in Tenda i9 1.0.0.5(2204). This vulnerability affects the function R7WebsSecurityHandlerfunction of the component HTTP Handler. The manipulation leads to path traversal. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit is publicly available and might be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to perform path traversal via the HTTP Handler component, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive files or system compromise. Remote exploitation is possible, and the exploit is publicly available, increasing the likelihood of attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply any available patches or firmware updates from Tenda to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices and limit exposure to external threats.
Monitor network traffic for signs of exploitation attempts and block suspicious activity using intrusion detection systems.

title: A vulnerability was detected in ByteDance coze-studio up to 0.5.1. Affected by this vulnerability...
description: A vulnerability was detected in ByteDance coze-studio up to 0.5.1. Affected by this vulnerability is the function ExecuteSQL of the file backend/domain/memory/database/service/database_impl.go of the component databaseTool. Performing a manipulation results in sql injection. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to perform SQL injection via the ExecuteSQL function in the databaseTool component, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data leakage, or manipulation of sensitive information.
recommended_actions: Immediately update ByteDance coze-studio to a patched version if available, or apply temporary mitigations such as input validation and sanitization.
Monitor systems for unusual database activity and implement logging to detect potential exploitation attempts.
Restrict access to the vulnerable component and database until the issue is resolved.

title: A vulnerability was found in MaxSite CMS up to 109.3. Impacted is an unknown function of the...
description: A vulnerability was found in MaxSite CMS up to 109.3. Impacted is an unknown function of the component ushki Plugin. Performing a manipulation of the argument f_ushka_new/f_ushk results in cross site scripting. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been made public and could be used. Upgrading to version 109.4 is recommended to address this issue. The patch is named 8a3946bd0a54bfb72a4d57179fcd253f2c550cd7. Upgrading the affected component is recommended. The vendor was

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the ushki Plugin of MaxSite CMS up to version 109.3 allows for cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks, which can lead to unauthorized access, data theft, or malicious code execution in the context of the affected users.
recommended_actions: Upgrade MaxSite CMS to version 109.4 or apply the patch 8a3946bd0a54bfb72a4d57179fcd253f2c550cd7 immediately.
Conduct a security review of all plugins and custom scripts to ensure no other vulnerabilities are present.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious XSS payloads as a temporary mitigation.

title: CVE-2026-6576 - A vulnerability was determined in liangliangyy DjangoBlog up to 2.1.0.0. The affected element is the
description: A vulnerability was determined in liangliangyy DjangoBlog up to 2.1.0.0. The affected element is the function CommandHandler of the file servermanager/api/commonapi.py of the component WeChat Bot Interface. Executing a manipulation of the argument Source can lead to command injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via the WeChat Bot Interface, potentially compromising the server and enabling unauthorized access or control. This could lead to data breaches, service disruptions, or further exploitation within the affected environment.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to a patched version of liangliangyy DjangoBlog if available, or apply temporary mitigations such as disabling the vulnerable WeChat Bot Interface.
Restrict access to the servermanager/api/commonapi.py file and validate all inputs to prevent command injection vulnerabilities.
Monitor server logs for unusual activity, particularly related to the WeChat Bot Interface, and implement intrusion detection systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: CVE-2026-0868 - The EMC – Easily Embed Calendly Scheduling Features plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cro
description: The EMC – Easily Embed Calendly Scheduling Features plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the plugin's calendly shortcode in all versions up to, and including, 4.4 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on user supplied attributes. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-0868.
